articles.title: NCLT Approves ₹17.25 Cr Resolution Plan After CoC Endorsement & Regulatory Compliance
articles.summary: The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) has approved a ₹17.25 crore resolution plan for a company after it received endorsement from the Committee of Creditors (CoC) and met regulatory compliance requirements. The NCLT clarified that exemptions for taxes, duties, and statutory compliances must still be obtained separately from competent authorities, as the approval does not waive statutory liabilities. This ruling underscores the legal and financial implications of insolvency resolution process
articles.raw_summary: While approving the resolution plan, NCLT clarified that exemptions relating to taxes, duties, and statutory compliances must be obtained from the competent authorities separately. Approval of the plan does not itself waive statutory liabilities. The post NCLT Approves ₹17.25 Cr Resolution Plan After CoC Endorsement   Regulatory Compliance appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: SC Leave Fare Concession Ruling: ITAT Holds Bank Liable for TDS Default
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that a bank could be held liable for failing to deduct Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) on Leave Fare Concession (LFC) for foreign travel, following the Supreme Court's decision denying LFC exemptions. The ITAT stated that no court order restrained the bank from deducting TDS, making it an 'assessee in default.' This ruling has implications for tax compliance and financial regulations in India.
articles.raw_summary: The ITAT held that, following the Supreme Court's ruling denying LFC exemption for foreign travel, a bank could be treated as an assessee in default where no court order restrained TDS deduction. The post SC Leave Fare Concession Ruling: ITAT Holds Bank Liable for TDS Default appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Service Tax Not Payable under Support Service as Railways Merely Licensed Advertisement Space
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that payments made by advertisers to Indian Railways for exclusive use of advertisement spaces do not attract service tax under the 'support service' category. Instead, the transaction was treated as renting immovable property, falling under forward charge provisions. This decision clarifies tax liability for such agreements and impacts how similar transactions are taxed in the future.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal observed that payment of licence fees for exclusive use of demarcated spaces amounted to renting of immovable property. Consequently, the transaction attracted forward charge provisions rather than reverse charge liability. The post Service Tax Not Payable under Support Service as Railways Merely Licensed Advertisement Space appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: SC Upholds Deletion of Ad Hoc Expense Additions as Books Were Not Rejected
articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld the deletion of ad hoc expense additions by tax authorities, ruling that disallowances were unjustified since the taxpayer's books of account were accepted. The case involved disputed business and labor expenses despite no rejection of the books. The decision reinforces the principle that tax authorities cannot make arbitrary additions when books are deemed reliable. This ruling impacts tax assessment practices and could influence future disputes over expense disallowanc
articles.raw_summary: The case involved disallowances of business and labour expenses despite accepted books of account. The Supreme Court declined to interfere with the findings deleting the additions. The post SC Upholds Deletion of Ad Hoc Expense Additions as Books Were Not Rejected appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: SC upholds NGT order that landlord cannot be liable for environmental violations committed by tenant
articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld a National Green Tribunal (NGT) order stating that a landlord cannot be held liable for environmental violations committed by their tenant. The ruling clarifies legal responsibility in cases of environmental damage, emphasizing that landlords are not automatically accountable for their tenants' actions. This decision has implications for property ownership, environmental compliance, and legal liabilities in India.

articles.title: Gauhati HC Quashes GST Cancellation Due to Wrong Form & Denial of 30-Day Response Period
articles.summary: The Gauhati High Court quashed the GST cancellation of a taxpayer because the tax department used the wrong form (GST REG-17) instead of the legally mandated form (GST REG-31) after suspending the registration. The court ruled that the denial of a 30-day response period to the taxpayer was also unsustainable. This ruling clarifies the correct procedure for addressing non-compliance related to bank account details under GST rules.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that alleged non-compliance relating to bank account details under Rule 10A must be addressed through Form GST REG-31 after suspension of registration. Use of Form GST REG-17 was found legally unsustainable. The post Gauhati HC Quashes GST Cancellation Due to Wrong Form   Denial of 30-Day Response Period appeared first on TaxGuru .

It emphasizes the need for global policy to align with these granular human incentives to effectively combat climate change.

articles.title: NCLT Clears LLP Merger While Preserving Tax Authorities’ Right to Examine Tax Avoidance Issues
articles.summary: The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) approved a Limited Liability Partnership (LLP) merger scheme but clarified that the Income Tax Department retains the right to examine any tax implications or potential tax avoidance arising from the arrangement. The tribunal did not block tax authorities from taking appropriate action under tax laws. This ruling ensures that tax scrutiny remains open despite the merger's approval.
articles.raw_summary: Although the Scheme was sanctioned, the Tribunal clarified that the Income Tax Department remained free to investigate any tax implications arising from the arrangement. Appropriate action under tax laws was expressly kept open. The post NCLT Clears LLP Merger While Preserving Tax Authorities’ Right to Examine Tax Avoidance Issues appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Govt OMCs Hike Jet Fuel By 10% To ₹115/Litre Under New Price Stabilisation Scheme
articles.summary: Government-owned oil marketing companies (OMCs) increased jet fuel prices by 10% to ₹115 per litre under a new price stabilization scheme. Airlines opting into the scheme will have locked-in rates for up to three years, while non-participating airlines will pay market-linked prices (~₹142/litre). The scheme aims to stabilize fuel costs for participating airlines. The government's involvement indicates a policy-driven intervention in the aviation fuel market.
articles.raw_summary: The new rate will be locked in for up to three years for airlines that opt to participate in the government-backed price stabilisation scheme. However, airlines that do not opt for the scheme will pay market-linked prices, which are currently around Rs 142 per litre, similar to international carriers.

articles.title: Section 138 NI Act Proceedings Continue Despite Personal Insolvency Moratorium: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that prosecutions under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments (NI) Act for cheque dishonour cannot be halted by personal insolvency moratoriums under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC). The Court held that such cases are criminal in nature and continue despite insolvency protections. This decision clarifies the legal framework governing financial disputes and insolvency proceedings in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court examined whether personal insolvency proceedings under the IBC could halt cheque dishonour cases under Section 138 of the NI Act. The Court held that such prosecutions are criminal in nature and continue despite insolvency moratorium protections. articles.title: ‘Those AI Solutions Must Have Their Foundations in Our Own Laws’: Sir Geoffrey Vos on AI, Digital Sovereignty and the Future of Arbitration at ICA’s 4th Indo-UK Conference
articles.summary: Sir Geoffrey Vos, in a keynote at the ICA’s 4th Indo-UK Conference, warned that AI systems used in arbitral decisions must be grounded in local laws to avoid unenforceable or biased outcomes. He distinguished between AI in arbitration (where party consent allows adoption) and AI in courts (where constitutional legitimacy is a barrier). The discussion underscores the need for AI solutions to align with national legal frameworks.
articles.raw_summary: If the AI systems driving arbitral decisions are trained on materials from other jurisdictions, the awards they generate may be unenforceable or worse, systematically biased. Sir Geoffrey Vos used his keynote at the ICA's 4th Indo-UK Commercial Disputes Conference to draw a line between AI in arbitration, where party consent makes adoption feasible, and AI in courts, where constitutional legitimacy makes it impossible. The post  Those AI Solutions Must Have Their Foundations in Our Own Law

articles.title: CESTAT Sets Aside Customs Broker Penalty as Ergotamine Preparation Not Covered Under NDPS Schedule-B
articles.summary: The Customs, Excise and Service Tax Appellate Tribunal (CESTAT) set aside a penalty imposed on a customs broker for importing an Ergotamine-based medicine. The tribunal ruled that the medicine was a preparation and not the banned substance 'Ergotamine and its salts' under Schedule-B of the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances (NDPS) Order, meaning no No Objection Certificate (NOC) was required. The penalty was thus quashed.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that a medicine containing Ergotamine was merely a preparation and not “Ergotamine and its salts” under Schedule-B of the NDPS Order. As no NOC was required, the penalty imposed on the Customs Broker was quashed. The post CESTAT Sets Aside Customs Broker Penalty as Ergotamine Preparation Not Covered Under NDPS Schedule-B appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Duplicate SIM exposes a structural flaw in UPI, Karnataka HC ruling shows

The court's observation underscores vulnerabilities in digital payment security and the need for stronger safeguards.

articles.title: Taxability of concessional or interest-free loan perquisite by Employee
articles.summary: The article explains that loans provided by employers at concessional or zero interest rates are considered taxable perquisites under Indian tax law. Employees benefit from the financial advantage of such loans, which are treated as part of their salary income. Exemptions exist for loans up to ₹20,000 and for specified medical treatment loans. The analysis is based on provisions of the Income Tax Act.
articles.raw_summary: Employer-provided interest-free or concessional loans are taxable as salary perquisites due to the financial benefit enjoyed by employees. Exemptions apply for loans up to ₹20,000 and specified medical treatment loans. The post Taxability of concessional or interest-free loan perquisite by Employee appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: End of Invisible Dyeing Costs: Compliance Demands Are Redefining the Economics of Textile Production
articles.summary: Textile dyeing, a water-intensive and polluting process, faces growing regulatory pressure globally due to water pollution and resource consumption. articles.raw_summary: Water pollution, resource consumption and regulatory scrutiny are converging on one of textile manufacturing's most established processes. Dyeing remains essential to fabric production, yet significant quantities of water, energy and chemicals are consumed in managing unfixed colourants. As compliance requirements tighten globally, manufacturers are reassessing how efficiency, wastewater management and competitiveness intersect.

The appeal was dismissed as a result. This ruling underscores the strict adherence to procedural timelines in consumer dispute redressal.

articles.title: Supreme Court Summarises Law On Approver's Testimony, Says Corroboration Not Mandatory For Accomplice's Evidence
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that an accomplice's testimony can form the basis of a conviction even without independent corroboration, provided the court finds it credible and truthful. The ruling clarifies that the testimony must be inculpatory (not exculpatory) and reflect a full account of the offense. This decision impacts how courts evaluate witness testimonies in criminal trials across India.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court observed that even where an accomplice's testimony is not independently corroborated on material particulars, it may still form the basis of a conviction if the court finds it credible, trustworthy, and reflective of a full and truthful account of the events surrounding the commission of the offence. “One of the necessary tests is that his (approver) testimony must be inculpatory and not exculpatory. articles.title: 2026 LiveLaw (SC) 607 | UPERNDRA KHARE VERSUS THE STATE OF MADHYA PRADESH
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled in the case of Upendra Khare versus the State of Madhya Pradesh that a hostile witness does not necessarily invalidate recovery if other evidence supports it. The judgment pertains to Section 27 of the Indian Evidence Act, emphasizing that recovery can be proven through alternative evidence even if a panch witness turns hostile. This ruling clarifies legal standards for evidence in criminal cases.

articles.title: Demurrage Charges Not Taxable as They Are Contractual Transportation Adjustments & Not Consideration for Services
articles.summary: The Economic, Statistical and Administrative Tribunal (ESTAT) ruled that demurrage charges linked to transportation contracts are not taxable as they constitute contractual adjustments rather than consideration for services. This decision clarifies that such charges cannot be taxed under 'Port Services' or 'declared services'. The ruling impacts how logistics and port-related transactions are treated for tax purposes in India.
articles.raw_summary: ESTAT held demurrage linked to transportation contracts is not consideration for services and cannot be taxed under Port Services or declared services. This ruling impacts tax liability assessments under the Minimum Alternate Tax (MAT) framework, reinforcing the importance of thorough initial scrutiny in tax cases.

articles.title: S.27 Evidence Act | Panch Witness Turning Hostile Not Fatal If Recovery Proved By Other Evidence: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld a murder conviction, ruling that a panch witness turning hostile does not necessarily weaken the prosecution's case if recovery evidence is proven by other corroborative means. The bench of Justices Pankaj Mithal and Prasanna B. Varale affirmed the lower courts' findings in a case from Madhya Pradesh. The ruling clarifies that disclosure statements under Section 27 of the Evidence Act can still support convictions even if witness testimony falters.
articles.raw_summary: Section 27 Evidence Act The Supreme Court recently upheld the murder conviction, noting that mere turning hostile of the panch witness would not be fatal to the prosecution's case to create doubt over the recovery evidence based on the accused's disclosure statements under Section 27 of the Evidence Act when the recovery evidence was proved otherwise by other corroborative evidence. A bench of Justice Pankaj Mithal and Justice Prasanna B. Varale affirmed the concurrent findings of the Madhya Pra

articles.title: Homebuyer’s Anti-Profiteering Complaint Rejected as Project Was Entirely in GST Regime
articles.summary: The Goods and Services Tax Appellate Tribunal (GSTAT) rejected an anti-profiteering complaint by a homebuyer after reviewing findings from the Directorate General of Anti-Profiteering (DGAP). The tribunal found that since the project was entirely under the GST regime and no additional Input Tax Credit (ITC) benefit accrued, no profiteering was established. The ruling clarifies the application of GST anti-profiteering rules in real estate projects.
articles.raw_summary: GSTAT accepted the DGAP finding that the ratio of credit availed to purchase value declined after GST implementation. Since no additional ITC benefit accrued, no profiteering was established. The post Homebuyer’s Anti-Profiteering Complaint Rejected as Project Was Entirely in GST Regime appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Ujjwala Yojana: Subsidised LPG cylinders capped at 4
articles.summary: The government has reduced the number of subsidized LPG cylinders under the Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) from nine to four per year for beneficiaries. PMUY, launched in 2016, provides free LPG connections to adult women from poor households. This change impacts millions of households relying on subsidized fuel for cooking. The decision follows earlier reductions from 12 to nine cylinders last year.
articles.raw_summary: Under the Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY), launched in May 2016 to provide deposit-free LPG connections to adult women from poor households, beneficiaries were initially entitled to 12 subsidised 14.2-kg cylinders a year. The subsidised quota was reduced to nine cylinders last year and has now been cut further to four.

articles.title: The move into gold by other nations has happened before. It’s bad news for US dollar
articles.summary: Central banks worldwide are reducing their holdings of U.S. assets in favor of gold, a trend last seen in 1971 when nations moved away from dollars before the U.S. suspended gold convertibility. The shift reflects concerns about the dollar's stability and could impact global financial systems. For India, this may influence foreign exchange reserves, trade strategies, and economic policies as the country navigates a changing monetary landscape.
articles.raw_summary: The recent shift from U.S. assets to gold mirrors 1971, when central banks dumped dollars before President Nixon ended gold convertibility.

articles.summary: The government introduced a price stabilization scheme for aviation turbine fuel (ATF), locking in rates at Rs 115 per litre for airlines for up to three years. The scheme is voluntary and involves multiple stakeholders, including the government, oil retailers, and airlines.

The policy is part of broader efforts to prevent technology leaks and maintain strategic control over cutting-edge semiconductor exports.
articles.raw_summary: Taiwan is tightening the reins on AI chip sales to China, aligning its regulations with those of the United States. This strategic maneuver aims to block the export of cutting-edge technologies, particularly AI servers powered by Nvidia chips, from Taiwan to its neighbor. With this heightened oversight, authorities are gearing up to prevent any potential technology leaks.

articles.raw_summary: IFSCA clarified that a common brand name by itself is sufficient to establish a Group Entity relationship under the ship leasing framework. articles.title: No Forgery Merely Because Person Executed Document Wrongly Claiming Ownership Of Property  : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that executing a document incorrectly claiming ownership of property does not constitute forgery under Sections 464, 467, or 471 of the Indian Penal Code. The court clarified that such an act does not meet the legal definition of making a false document or forgery, thereby absolving the individual of criminal liability in this context.
articles.raw_summary: Execution of such a document is not "making of false document(S.464 IPC)" and hence no offence is "forgery(S.467/471 IPC)" is attracted.

articles.title: ‘50-year demand’ nears fulfilment as Zojila tunnel breakthrough brings Ladakh closer to all-weather connectivity
articles.summary: The Zojila tunnel, a key infrastructure project in Ladakh, has achieved a breakthrough after a 50-year demand for all-weather connectivity. The tunnel, connecting Srinagar to Leh, is being developed by the Border Roads Organisation (BRO) under the Ministry of Defence. This project aims to improve year-round access to Ladakh, which is currently cut off during winter due to heavy snowfall. The completion of the tunnel is expected to boost regional connectivity and strategic mobility.

articles.title: OECD cuts global growth forecast, warns prolonged US-Iran war could trigger recessions
articles.summary: The OECD reduced its global growth forecast, warning that a prolonged US-Iran war in West Asia could disrupt shipping and damage energy infrastructure, slowing global growth to 2.1% in 2026 and 1.8% in 2027. The organization cautioned that such disruptions could have severe consequences, including potential recessions. The scenario assumes continued conflicts into 2027, affecting energy supplies and trade routes.
articles.raw_summary: OECD cautioned that a prolonged West Asia conflict would have far more severe consequences. Under a scenario where shipping disruptions and damage to energy infrastructure continue into 2027, global growth could slow to 2.1% in 2026 and 1.8% in 2027.

articles.title: Solar power curtailment raises red flags for renewable investors
articles.summary: On 1 May, nearly one-sixth of India's 150GW solar capacity was curtailed, likely due to low industrial demand during Labour Day. Experts suggest the shutdown of industries for the holiday contributed to the excess power supply. The incident raises concerns for renewable energy investors about grid management and policy support for solar power. The government or regulators may need to address these challenges to ensure stable renewable energy growth.
articles.raw_summary: The solar capacity that was curtailed on 1 May equals almost one-sixth of the country’s installed capacity of 150GW. Experts said that low demand due to industries closing for Labour Day on 1 May may have contributed to the curtailment.

articles.title: Ashok Gehlot breaks silence on Sachin Pilot: ‘I facilitated his rise, but he never acknowledged it’
articles.summary: Former Rajasthan Chief Minister Ashok Gehlot publicly criticized Sachin Pilot, alleging that he played a key role in Pilot's appointment to the Union Cabinet but received no acknowledgment. Gehlot expressed personal hurt over Pilot's lack of gratitude despite his support. The dispute highlights internal party tensions within the Congress, which could influence future political strategies or governance decisions.

articles.title: ‘Grave suspicion against accused’—PMLA court junks CM Hemant Soren’s discharge plea in land parcel case
articles.summary: A special court in Jharkhand rejected Chief Minister Hemant Soren's plea to be discharged in a land parcel case under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA). Soren is currently out on bail.

articles.title: 3 Kashmiri students sentenced, Zakir Musa ‘kingpin’ acquitted. Court rap to NIA for ‘miserable failures’

articles.title: HC junks contempt plea, upholds right of press to report on court order before it’s signed by judge
articles.summary: A division bench of the High Court dismissed a contempt plea against three newspapers that reported on an oral court order before it was formally signed by the judge. The single-judge bench had previously initiated contempt proceedings against the newspapers for reporting the oral order. The division bench upheld the press's right to report on court proceedings, reinforcing media freedom and the public's right to information.
articles.raw_summary: Three newspapers got clean chit from division bench after single-judge bench took exception to reporting of oral court order   initiated contempt proceedings.

The CBSE is a government regulatory body overseeing school education in India.

articles.title: No mobile internet in parts of Jaipur today ahead of JDA encroachment drive
articles.summary: The Jaipur Development Authority (JDA) has suspended mobile internet, social media, and bulk messaging services in parts of Jaipur until June 9 ahead of an anti-encroachment drive. The suspension is justified by concerns over potential law and order issues and misinformation. The move affects communication and digital access for residents in the affected areas.

articles.title: Indus Waters Treaty: Centre says Pakistan won't get 'a single drop' in coming years
articles.summary: The Indian government stated that Pakistan will not receive any water from the Indus Waters Treaty in the coming years. The Centre's announcement reflects a firm stance on water-sharing with Pakistan, highlighting India's control over the treaty's provisions. articles.summary: Newly elected Maharashtra Legislative Council member Vikram Kakade has clarified that the ₹10 crore listed against Jay Pawar in his election affidavit was an advance payment for a land transaction and not a loan. The Pune-based builder, who was elected unopposed from the Pune local authorities constituency, disclosed family assets worth more than ₹150 crore, including luxury vehicles, investments, and real estate holdings in India and Dubai.

articles.title: Chinese hackers pose biggest espionage threat to tech firms, CrowdStrike says
articles.summary: Chinese-linked hackers posed the largest espionage threat to technology firms in the past year, according to cybersecurity firm CrowdStrike. The surge in cyber threats coincides with increased investments in artificial intelligence. North Korea, Russia, and Iran-linked hacking groups also targeted tech firms, alongside financially motivated cybercriminals. The technology sector remains a prime target for foreign governments and cybercriminals.

The technology sector remains a prime target for foreign governments and cybercriminals. North Korean, Russian, and Iranian hacking groups also pose threats. Financially motivated cybercriminals also increased their targeting of tech firms.

articles.title: Infineon taps CDIL, Kaynes to strengthen India chip collab
articles.summary: German chipmaker Infineon Technologies is expanding its engagement with India's semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem by partnering with CDIL Semiconductors and Kaynes Semicon. The company is transferring packaging expertise and plans to procure packaged components from these Indian firms, supporting the nation's ambition to localize chip production.

articles.title: US says BYD, Baidu and Alibaba are aiding China's military
articles.summary: The US Pentagon released a list of Chinese companies, including Alibaba, Baidu, BYD, WuXi AppTec, and RoboSense Technology, accused of supporting China's military. The Pentagon will stop contracting with these firms in the future but has not imposed immediate sanctions. The move highlights growing US-China tech and military tensions, which could impact global supply chains and India's strategic considerations.
articles.raw_summary: America has released a new list of Chinese companies it believes support Beijing's military. Major firms like Alibaba, Baidu, and BYD are included. Other companies like WuXi AppTec and RoboSense Technology are also on the list. While not imposing immediate sanctions, the Pentagon will stop contracting with these firms in the future.

articles.title: China's global ecommerce push stalls as Iran war lifts costs, dampens demand
articles.summary: China's low-cost e-commerce model, which relies on shipping cheap goods globally, is facing challenges due to rising costs from the Middle East conflict and existing U.S. tariffs. Logistics companies like DHL Express have imposed fuel surcharges, further straining the business model. The situation reflects broader trade disruptions that could impact global supply chains and e-commerce dynamics.
articles.raw_summary: The business models, based on flying $5 dresses from Chinese factories to shoppers around the world, were already under pressure after U.S. President Donald Trump introduced tariffs and axed customs waivers on low-value parcels last year. Soaring logistics costs stemming ‌from the Middle East ⁠conflict are adding ⁠to the strain, data shows and industry insiders say, with shippers like DHL Express imposing hefty fuel surcharges.

articles.title: India's cybersecurity talent gap grows as skills prove hard to find
articles.summary: India faces a critical cybersecurity talent shortage, with nearly 39,000 unfilled positions, especially in emerging fields like IoT and blockchain. High attrition rates and increasing cyberattacks exacerbate the issue. New regulations demand greater compliance, while AI alone cannot bridge the gap, highlighting the need for skilled professionals and educational reforms.

New regulations demand greater compliance, while AI alone cannot bridge the gap, highlighting the need for skilled professionals and educational reforms.

articles.title: Here to win quick commerce, will become market leader in near-term: Samir Kumar, Amazon India chief
articles.summary: Amazon is planning to aggressively expand its 10-minute delivery service, Amazon Now, aiming for market leadership despite moderating quick commerce growth. The ecommerce giant plans to launch in over 100 cities with 1,000 dark stores, emphasising customer experience, wide selection, and speed to compete with rivals like Blinkit and Zepto.

articles.title: Rupee slide pushes overseas students back to lenders for top-up loans
articles.summary: A significant rupee depreciation has forced Indian students studying abroad to seek top-up loans from lenders, as initial loan amounts fall short of rising tuition and living expenses. This has led to a nearly threefold increase in demand for additional funding, with students requiring Rs 1-6 lakh more.

Exporters are blending tradition with technology to reach international markets.

articles.title: Zojila tunnel breakthrough to bring Ladakh closer to all-weather access
articles.summary: The Zojila Tunnel project, a 13.153-km tunnel connecting Kashmir and Ladakh, has achieved a breakthrough. This infrastructure project, led by the government, will provide all-weather road access for the first time, improving troop mobility and reducing travel time. The tunnel is part of broader efforts to enhance connectivity in the region.

articles.title: India exports 1-tonne of GI-tagged Tezpur Litchi to Dubai
articles.summary: New Delhi has exported one tonne of GI-tagged Tezpur Litchi from Assam to Dubai. This marks a significant step in promoting the premium fruit in overseas markets. The Commerce Ministry is actively promoting agri products from the North Eastern Region. The Geographical Indication status has boosted Tezpur Litchi's identity and market recognition.

articles.title: Indian economy, government finances, see mounting costs from Iran war
articles.summary: India's economy, once humming with benign inflation and steady growth, now faces mounting costs from the Iran war. As a major oil importer, disruptions to supply and elevated prices are impacting growth, inflation, and government finances. The central bank's ability to manage these shocks is increasingly difficult.

articles.title: El Niño clouds loom over kharif crops, farm pay
articles.summary: A developing El Niño event thousands of miles away poses a significant threat to India's agriculture. This weather phenomenon is linked to weaker monsoon rains and higher temperatures. Experts predict a potential decline in kharif crop production, impacting oilseeds and pulses. This could lead to rising inflation and reduced earnings for farmers across the country.

articles.title: Exports rise 15 per cent during April-May, government official says
articles.summary: India's merchandise exports show strong growth. Exports rose by 15 percent in April-May 2026-27. This happened despite global economic uncertainties. Petroleum products drove April's outbound shipments. This was the highest in over four years. However, the trade deficit widened. Imports also saw an uptick. The Commerce Ministry will release May's trade data soon.

articles.summary: India's electronics manufacturing is on the rise. Last year, components worth 35,000 crore rupees were exported to China. India is moving up the value chain, designing and manufacturing complex products.

Foreign investors are wary due to weak earnings growth relative to India's premium valuation.

articles.title: AI not a threat, but opportunity for IT services: TCS chairman N Chandrasekaran
articles.summary: TCS chairman N Chandrasekaran stated that AI is not a threat to traditional IT services but rather a significant opportunity for the IT industry. He emphasized that AI could drive growth for companies like TCS and the broader sector. The remarks were made in response to concerns about AI reducing demand for IT services.
articles.raw_summary: Addressing the concerns that AI could reduce demand for traditional IT services, Chandrasekaran said the market has misunderstood the relationship between AI and technology services. "Far from being a mortal threat, AI is the most significant opportunity yet for TCS and, in fact, for the IT industry as a whole," he said.

articles.title: Intel spinout Articul8 eyes $50 million for India heritage AI venture
articles.summary: Articul8, a spin-off from Intel, is raising $30-50 million to establish an India-based AI entity focused on traditional knowledge systems like Sanskrit. The venture aims to build "heritage models" trained on ancient Indian languages and cultural data, with potential applications in various fields and global preservation efforts.

The venture aims to build "heritage models" trained on ancient Indian languages and cultural data, with potential applications in various fields and global preservation efforts.

articles.title: Competition Commission clears ANTA Sports' proposal to acquire 29% stake in PUMA

The deal was previously agreed upon between ANTA Sports and Groupe Artemis, the investment arm of the Pinault family. The CCI's clearance is a regulatory step that ensures compliance with competition laws in India.

articles.title: Oil prices slide below $91 as Israel-Iran truce eases supply fears
articles.summary: Brent crude oil prices fell below $91 per barrel as tensions between Israel and Iran eased, reducing supply concerns. Weak demand from China and rising crude exports from the US also contributed to the price drop. The article highlights how these global factors could impact India's energy costs and policy considerations, despite a modest increase in OPEC+ oil production.

articles.title: ATF prices hiked 10% as oil firms launch long-term price stabilisation plan
articles.summary: The Indian government has increased ATF (Aviation Turbine Fuel) prices by 10% as part of a new long-term price stabilization plan. ATF constitutes 35-40% of an airline's operating costs, and the plan aims to provide airlines with cost visibility while protecting consumers from price volatility. Oil firms are involved in implementing this stabilization measure.
articles.raw_summary: ATF accounts for nearly 35-40% of an airline’s operating costs, making fuel price volatility one of the biggest challenges for carriers. By offering a fixed-price option, the government hopes to provide airlines greater visibility on costs and protect consumers from higher prices.

This initiative reflects China's push to dominate the AI sector and could impact global tech supply chains and India's policy decisions in AI and semiconductor development.

articles.title: Explained: How AI mania has thrown India Inc out of MSCI EM's top 10 and world's top 100 list
articles.summary: Indian companies are no longer in the top 10 of the MSCI Emerging Markets Index. A surge in AI stocks has shifted global capital. Taiwan and South Korea's chipmakers now dominate the index. Reliance Industries and HDFC Bank have fallen in global rankings. India's market weight has hit a six-year low. This concentration is driven by strong company growth.
